summary: Ukrainian forces report high‑intensity UAV activity across multiple fronts, with dozens of Shahed attacks on northern and central oblasts and significant Russian drone strikes on Dnipro and other southern cities. Russian air defenses claim to have downed over 279 drones, while Ukrainian air defenses report shooting down 138 Russian UAVs over Russian territory. Casualties from drone strikes continue to rise, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv regions.
key_points: Ukrainian Shahed drones hit multiple locations in Chernihiv, Sumy, Poltava, and Cherkasy.
Russian UAV attacks caused fires and casualties in Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv.
Ukrainian air defense downed 279 of 287 Russian drones in one night.
Russian forces claim to have shot down 138 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions.
Heavy drone activity reported on the southern front near Krivoy Rog and Odesa.
Multiple civilian casualties reported, including a 38‑year‑old man killed in Dnipropetrovsk.
Both sides continue extensive UAV operations targeting infrastructure and troop concentrations.

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian Shahed drone swarms intensified across the north and central fronts, striking Chernihiv, Sumy, Poltava, and Cherkasy, while Russian UAV attacks on Dnipro and southern cities caused fires and civilian casualties. Ukrainian air defenses reported downing 279 of 287 Russian drones in a single night, yet Russian forces claim 138 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory. The surge in UAV activity raises the risk of further civilian harm and complicates front‑line operations.

points: Shahed groups (2‑6 units) were observed over Chernihiv (Dobrянка, Bakhmach, Borzna), Sumy (Esman, Vorozhba), Poltava (Opishnia) and Cherkasy, indicating coordinated northern‑central strike patterns (Source: @eRadarrua/92161).

Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed attack on Dnipro causing fires and three injuries; total regional injuries reached eight, with additional strikes in Zaporizhzhia (seven injured) and Kharkiv (no casualties) (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).

A large Ukrainian UAV sortie hit Dnipropetrovsk region over 70 times with artillery and rockets, killing a 38‑year‑old man and wounding nine, including children, and damaging infrastructure such as schools and gas stations (Source: @UkraineNow/81260).
Ukrainian air defense claimed to have neutralised 279 of 287 Russian drones during the night, with eight strike drones recorded hitting seven locations (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).

Multiple Shahed attacks were reported on the southern front: a group heading south of Kryvyi Rih with a western course, and a Shahed over Odesa sea heading toward Lyman and Odesa (Source: @eRadarrua/92141).
Ukrainian intelligence noted new UAV groups moving toward Apostolove from the south in Dnipropetrovsk oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/92121).
Ukrainian forces observed Shahed activity over Kyiv region, including a UAV near Volodarka heading toward Zhytomyr and another near Romodan heading west (Source: @eRadarrua/92142).
Russian UAV attacks on Moscow were described as the largest in over a year, according to TASS counts (Source: @tass_agency/376225).
Russian forces reported shooting down 138 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions, including Stavropol, Crimea, Rostov, and Belgorod (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).
A Russian drone strike damaged four private houses and three cars in Sergiev Posad, Moscow region (Source: @tass_agency/376226).
Russian air defenses claimed successful interceptions of Ukrainian Shahed drones over the northern front, though specific numbers were not disclosed (inferred from Ukrainian loss reports).
Russian artillery and missile strikes were reported on Izmail (Odesa), Kharkiv, Poltava, and Kryvyi Rih, indicating continued deep‑strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).
Russian UAVs were observed over the southern front heading west from the Black Sea towards Liman and Odesa, suggesting maritime launch points (Source: @eRadarrua/92141).
Russian forces reported a UAV‑caused fire and minor damage in the Moscow suburb of Sergiev Posad, highlighting continued use of drones against civilian targets (Source: @tass_agency/376226).

Ukrainian Shahed drones were reported over Sumy (Novoselytsia and two over a lake) and Chernihiv (3 near Nizhyn, 3 near Kulykivka) indicating pressure on the northeastern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/92156, @eRadarrua/92123).
Russian UAVs were shot down over the Sumy front, with Ukrainian reports of Shahed groups heading toward Poltava from Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62060).
Ukrainian forces noted UAV activity on the east of Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Dnipro, suggesting attempts to disrupt logistics (Source: @kpszsu/62096).
Russian reports mention a UAV strike on a cultural center in Shakhtarsk (DPR) causing casualties, reflecting cross‑border UAV operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).
Ukrainian units reported UAVs over the eastern Dnipropetrovsk area moving toward the city, potentially targeting the Dnipro River crossing points (Source: @kpszsu/62096).
Ukrainian Shahed attacks were logged in the Luhansk‑adjacent area of Solone, moving toward Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66770).
Russian forces claimed UAV engagements over the Sumy region, with mortar crews and UAV operators redeployed deeper into Ukrainian territory (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).

Multiple Shahed groups (2‑6 units) were observed over Chernihiv oblast locations including Dobrянка, Bakhmach, Borzna, and Nizhyn, indicating sustained pressure on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92161, @eRadarrua/92156).
Ukrainian reports listed UAVs over Chernihiv heading south‑west, and over Kyiv region heading toward the capital, suggesting attempts to breach air defenses (Source: @kpszsu/62077, @kpszsu/62060).
Russian UAV activity over Moscow was described as the largest in a year, reflecting a strategic focus on the north‑central Russian heartland (Source: @tass_agency/376225).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed over Poltava from the north, possibly launched from Chernihiv or Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62060).
Ukrainian air defense claimed 279 drone interceptions, many likely over the northern sector protecting Kyiv and Chernihiv (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Russian forces reported shooting down Ukrainian UAVs over the Stavropol and Crimean north, indicating cross‑border UAV engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a UAV near Romodan (Poltava) heading west, potentially targeting supply lines in the north‑west corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62060).

Russian UAV attacks on Dnipro caused fires, three injuries, and contributed to eight total regional injuries; additional strikes hit Zaporizhzhia (seven injured) and Kharkiv (no casualties) (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Ukrainian Shahed attacks were reported south of Kryvyi Rih with a western course, and a maritime UAV heading toward Liman and Odesa (Source: @eRadarrua/92141).
Russian forces claimed strikes on Izmail (Odesa region), Kharkiv, Poltava, and Kryvyi Rih, indicating coordinated southern offensive actions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).
Ukrainian reports listed a Shahed over Odesa region heading toward Berezivka, and a UAV over Mykolaiv region from the south (Source: @kpszsu/62081).
Civilian casualties in Dnipropetrovsk included a 38‑year‑old man killed and nine wounded, with infrastructure damage to schools, gas stations, and residential buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81260).
Ukrainian air defenses reported downing 279 of 287 Russian drones, many likely operating over the southern front to support ground attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Russian UAVs were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Liman and Odesa, suggesting maritime launch platforms (Source: @eRadarrua/92141).
Ukrainian air defense reported neutralising 279 of 287 Russian drones in one night, with eight strike drones recorded hitting seven locations (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Russian forces claimed to have shot down 138 Ukrainian UAVs across multiple regions, including Stavropol, Crimea, Rostov, and Belgorod (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).
TASS reported the largest Russian UAV attack on Moscow in over a year, indicating an escalation of aerial operations against Russian urban centers (Source: @tass_agency/376225).
Ukrainian reports listed numerous Shahed swarms across northern, central, and southern oblasts, demonstrating extensive UAV deployment by Ukraine (Source: multiple eRadarrua posts).
Russian UAV strikes damaged civilian structures in Sergiev Posad (Moscow region), showing continued use of drones against non‑military targets (Source: @tass_agency/376226).
Ukrainian forces observed UAVs over the Black Sea heading toward Liman and Odesa, suggesting maritime launch capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/92141).
Both sides reported significant UAV losses, indicating a high‑intensity drone war that is shaping ground operations across all fronts.

key_developments: Ukrainian Shahed swarms struck multiple northern and central locations including Chernihiv (Dobrянka, Bakhmach, Borzna) and Poltava (Opishnia), indicating coordinated offensive UAV use (Source: @eRadarrua/92161).
Russian UAV attack on Moscow was the largest in over a year, reflecting heightened Russian aerial aggression toward the capital (Source: @tass_agency/376225).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 279 of 287 Russian drones in a single night, demonstrating strong anti‑drone capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).
Russian forces reported shooting down 138 Ukrainian UAVs across Stavropol, Crimea, Rostov, and Belgorod, showing extensive Russian air defense activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211).
Drone strikes in Dnipro caused fires and three injuries; total regional injuries reached eight, with additional casualties in Zaporizhzhia (seven injured) (Source: @UkraineNow/81267).

Southern front saw Russian UAVs targeting Izmail, Kharkiv, Poltava and Kryvyi Rih, while Ukrainian Shahed drones operated south of Kryvyi Rih heading west (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30211, @eRadarrua/92141).
Civilian infrastructure in Dnipropetrovsk was heavily damaged by UAV attacks, killing a 38‑year‑old man and wounding nine, including children (Source: @UkraineNow/81260).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian Shahed drone deployments are intensifying across the north and central fronts, targeting logistics hubs and civilian centers, while Russian UAV strikes focus on strategic Russian cities and southern Ukrainian infrastructure. Both sides claim high UAV kill counts, indicating a saturated airspace where electronic warfare and point‑defence systems are heavily engaged. The continued use of maritime‑launched drones by Russia adds a new dimension to the southern threat envelope, potentially complicating Ukrainian naval and coastal defenses.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian regional commands have mobilised additional air‑defence assets, reporting successful interceptions but also acknowledging civilian casualties, prompting increased civil‑alert measures in Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia. Russian authorities are reinforcing air‑defence around Moscow and key southern cities, while also deploying ground forces to protect vulnerable infrastructure in the Black Sea coastal zones. Both governments are likely to request further external support for counter‑UAV technologies.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source UAV tracking indicates a shift toward higher‑density Shahed swarms in the north, suggesting preparation for larger ground offensives. Russian drone activity over Moscow may be a signaling effort ahead of potential escalatory operations. Maritime UAV launches toward Odesa hint at expanded Russian naval drone capabilities, warranting monitoring of Black Sea launch sites. Civilian casualty reports underscore the need for improved early‑warning and shelter protocols in high‑risk oblasts.
